note: Auto-generated with full schemas from tool decorators. Do not edit manually.

tools.description: Create a new access credential (NFC card, PIN, or mobile credential) and assign it to a user. Requires confirm=true to execute. Only available via local proxy session.
tools.name: access_create_credential

confirm.description: When true, creates the credential. When false (default), returns a preview.

credential_data.description: Credential payload. Required fields depend on type: nfc: {user_id, token}, pin: {user_id, pin_code}, mobile: {user_id}.

credential_type.description: Type of credential to create: nfc, pin, or mobile.

tools.description: Create a new visitor pass with a name and access time range. Optionally assign specific doors and contact information. Requires confirm=true to execute. Only available via local proxy session.
tools.name: access_create_visitor

access_end.description: End of access period as ISO 8601 timestamp (e.g., 2026-03-17T17:00:00Z)

access_start.description: Start of access period as ISO 8601 timestamp (e.g., 2026-03-17T09:00:00Z)

confirm.description: When true, creates the visitor pass. When false (default), returns a preview.

email.description: Visitor email address for notifications. Optional.

tools.description: Delete a visitor pass. This permanently removes the visitor's access. Requires confirm=true to execute. Only available via local proxy session.
tools.name: access_delete_visitor

confirm.description: When true, deletes the visitor pass. When false (default), returns a preview.

tools.description: Get an aggregated activity summary for Access events over a time period. Shows event counts, breakdowns by type, and trends. Optionally scope to a single door. Only available via local proxy session.
tools.name: access_get_activity_summary

days.description: Number of days to include in the summary (default 7).

door_id.description: Door UUID to scope the summary (from access_list_doors). Omit for all doors.

tools.description: Returns detailed information for a single access credential including type, status, assigned user, and creation date.
tools.name: access_get_credential

tools.description: Returns detailed information for a single Access device including name, type, connection state, firmware version, MAC, and IP address.
tools.name: access_get_device

tools.description: Returns detailed information for a single door including lock state, configuration, and connected devices.
tools.name: access_get_door

tools.description: Returns the current lock and position status for a single door. Lightweight alternative to access_get_door when you only need state info.
tools.name: access_get_door_status

tools.description: Returns detailed information for a single access event including event type, door, user, timestamp, and result.
tools.name: access_get_event

tools.description: Returns Access system health summary including API client and proxy session status. Use to verify connectivity and diagnose auth path issues.
tools.name: access_get_health

tools.description: Returns detailed information for a single access policy including assigned doors, schedule, user groups, and configuration.
tools.name: access_get_policy

tools.description: Returns Access controller model, firmware version, uptime, and connected device counts. Use for basic health checks and capacity overview.
tools.name: access_get_system_info

tools.description: Returns detailed information for a single visitor pass including name, access time range, assigned doors, and status.
tools.name: access_get_visitor

tools.description: Lists all access credentials (NFC cards, PINs, mobile credentials) with their type, status, and assigned user.
tools.name: access_list_credentials

tools.description: Lists all Access hardware devices (hubs, readers, relays, intercoms) with their name, type, connection state, and firmware version.
tools.name: access_list_devices

compact.description: When true, strips configs, images, location/door/floor duplicates, extensions, update_manual, and capabilities fields (~87% smaller). Recommended for overviews and summaries.

tools.description: Lists all door groups configured on the Access controller. Door groups organize doors for policy assignment. Only available via local proxy session.
tools.name: access_list_door_groups

tools.description: Lists all doors managed by the Access controller with their name, lock state, and connection status. Use to get an overview of all doors.
tools.name: access_list_doors

compact.description: When true, strips thumbnail, extras, and simplifies nested device details (~70% smaller). Recommended for overviews and summaries.

tools.description: Query access events from the controller with optional filters. Returns events such as door unlocks, access denials, and system events. Supports filtering by time range, door, user, and result limit. For real-time buffer events use access_recent_events.
tools.name: access_list_events

door_id.description: Filter events to a specific door by UUID (from access_list_doors). Omit for all doors.

end.description: End time as ISO 8601 timestamp (e.g., 2026-03-17T23:59:59Z). Omit for no upper bound.

limit.description: Maximum number of events to return (default 30).

start.description: Start time as ISO 8601 timestamp (e.g., 2026-03-17T00:00:00Z). Omit for no lower bound.

topic.description: Event topic to query: 'admin' (user/account changes) or 'admin_activity' (admin actions). Default: admin.

user_id.description: Filter events to a specific user by UUID (from access_list_users). Omit for all users.

tools.description: Lists all access policies configured on the Access controller. Shows policy name, assigned doors, schedules, and user groups.
tools.name: access_list_policies

tools.description: Lists all access schedules configured on the Access controller.
tools.name: access_list_schedules

tools.description: Lists all users registered in the Access controller with their access credentials and groups. Use to audit who has physical access or to look up a specific user.
tools.name: access_list_users

compact.description: When true, strips scopes, permissions, groups, roles, and resources fields (~85% smaller). Recommended for overviews and summaries.

limit.description: Maximum number of users to return. Omit for all users.

tools.description: Lists all visitor passes with their name, status, valid time range, and assigned doors.
tools.name: access_list_visitors

tools.description: Lock a door immediately. This is a physical real-world action. Requires confirm=true to execute. Only available via local proxy session.
tools.name: access_lock_door

confirm.description: When true, executes the lock. When false (default), returns a preview.

tools.description: Reboot an Access hardware device (hub, reader, relay, intercom). The device will be temporarily offline during reboot. Requires confirm=true to execute. Only available via local proxy session.
tools.name: access_reboot_device

confirm.description: When true, executes the reboot. When false (default), returns a preview.

tools.description: Get recent events from the in-memory websocket buffer. Fast (no API call). Supports filtering by event type and door. Use for real-time monitoring; use access_list_events for historical queries.
tools.name: access_recent_events

door_id.description: Filter events to a specific door by UUID (from access_list_doors). Omit for all doors.

event_type.description: Filter by event type: door_open, door_close, access_granted, access_denied, door_alarm.

limit.description: Maximum number of events to return from the buffer. Omit to return all buffered events.

tools.description: Revoke (delete) an access credential. This permanently removes the credential from the system. Requires confirm=true to execute. Only available via local proxy session.
tools.name: access_revoke_credential

confirm.description: When true, revokes the credential. When false (default), returns a preview.

tools.description: Returns instructions for subscribing to real-time Access events. Provides the MCP resource URI for the event stream and guidance on polling intervals. Use this to set up continuous event monitoring.
tools.name: access_subscribe_events

tools.description: Unlock a door for a specified duration. This is a physical real-world action. Requires confirm=true to execute. Default duration is 2 seconds.
tools.name: access_unlock_door

confirm.description: When true, executes the unlock. When false (default), returns a preview.

tools.description: Update an access policy's configuration. Supported fields include name, doors, schedule_id, and user groups. Requires confirm=true to apply. Only available via local proxy session.
tools.name: access_update_policy

changes.description: Dictionary of fields to update. Supported keys: name (string - policy display name), doors (list - door UUIDs to assign), schedule_id (string - schedule UUID to assign), user_groups (list - user group UUIDs).

confirm.description: When true, applies the update. When false (default), returns a preview.
